prompt: A whimsical high-detail oil-painting parody of Michelangelo’s Creation of Adam, but all human figures are replaced by teddy bears. On the left, a single plush teddy bear sits reclined on a rocky slope, posed like Adam, with soft golden-brown curly fur, round ears, shiny black eyes, a small dark nose, and a slightly open mouth. Its left arm supports its body while its right arm stretches outward, index paw extended toward the other bear. On the right, a larger central teddy bear posed like God emerges from a deep reddish-burgundy swirling mantle, wearing a flowing white draped robe, leaning forward with one outstretched arm and pointed paw reaching toward the left bear, almost touching fingertips. Surrounding the robed bear inside the mantle are 8 additional smaller teddy bears clustered tightly together, peeking from different angles like attendant figures. The composition exactly echoes the iconic near-touching hands scene, with the gap between fingertips centered. Use a cracked fresco wall background in warm beige tones, muted Renaissance palette, soft directional light, realistic fabric folds, detailed plush texture, subtle shadows, and a museum-quality painted surface. Blend classical Renaissance fresco aesthetics with charming surreal humor, keeping the scene elegant rather than cartoonish.
